title: [Bug]: PR label sync should only infer source issues from Related body references
summary: The label synchronization helper scans every non-code issue reference in a PR body, so ordinary prose can accidentally become a source issue for label copying.
observed: Any body text that contains a non-code #123 style reference can be treated as a source issue, even when the line does not identify managed implementation work.
expected: Body scanning should infer source issues only from explicit Related-style lines while still honoring explicit --issue arguments and branch-derived issue numbers.
reproduction: Run the label helper against a PR body that mentions an unrelated issue number in prose and label synchronization enabled.
failing_test: Add a unit test proving arbitrary prose references are ignored while Related #... lines are detected.
impact: Pull requests can inherit unrelated labels, reducing review and release filtering accuracy. Runtime delivery behavior is unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to GitHub workflow metadata automation and cannot affect sink commit or ACK behavior.
security: The fix should reduce accidental metadata propagation and must continue to ignore Markdown code spans and fenced code blocks.
acceptance: Arbitrary body prose such as See #123 is ignored for label source detection.
Related #123, #456 lines support multiple issue references.
Explicit issue arguments and branch-derived issues still work.
Unit tests cover the corrected detection policy.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update workflow documentation only if source issue syntax needs clarification.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
